question: How is Douyin's shift from livestream to shelf commerce progressing, and what competitive threat does it pose?

findings.claim: Douyin shelf commerce share grew from 30% (2023) to 40% (2024) to 50% (mid-2025), on track with 3-year plan
findings.data: Douyin's 3-year plan for shelf e-commerce: 30% in year 1 (2023, achieved), 40% in year 2 (2024, achieved), 50% in year 3 (2025, achieved by 618). Current GMV breakdown: shelf ~40-50%, merchant-led livestream ~30%, influencer-led livestream ~30%. Shelf-based e-commerce GMV grew 49% YoY (Aug 2024-Jul 2025), outpacing overall 34% growth.

findings.claim: Douyin e-commerce GMV reached ~3.5 trillion yuan in 2024, targeting 4.2 trillion in 2025; growth 34% YoY; approaching Pinduoduo scale
findings.data: 2024 GMV: ~3.5 trillion yuan (+30% YoY). 2025 target: 4.2 trillion yuan. Aug 2024-Jul 2025 growth: 34% YoY. First 10 months of 2025 exceeded 30% growth; annual GMV expected to surpass 4 trillion yuan. Douyin has become the third-largest e-commerce player behind Tmall and Pinduoduo.

findings.claim: Douyin's reliance on top-tier influencer live broadcasts has significantly decreased; merchant-led and smaller streamers replace them
findings.data: Reliance on top-tier influencer live broadcasts has significantly decreased, primarily due to rise of shelf-based shopping. In 2024, shelf-based shopping accounted for over 40% of GMV, store-hosted live broadcasts exceeded 30%, and remaining ~30% came from influencer live broadcasts. Top influencer streamers susceptible to controversies; merchant-led streaming and smaller streamers have become preferred replacements.

findings.claim: Douyin invested 32 billion yuan in merchant cost savings in 2025, including commission waivers, promotion fee rebates, and reduced shipping insurance
findings.data: 2025 cumulative merchant cost savings: >32 billion yuan. By August 2025, direct subsidies to merchants: >19 billion yuan. Q1 2025 alone: 5.5 billion yuan in cost reductions. Nine merchant support policies introduced early 2025: commission waivers for product cards, promotion fee rebates, reduced shipping insurance and security deposits, Small Merchant Support Fund. In March 2025, Ocean Engine (advertising arm) was merged into Douyin E-commerce. Two separate algorithm teams combined.

findings.claim: Douyin e-commerce's 2026 strategy focuses on further merchant support policy upgrades
findings.data: In 2026, Douyin E-commerce will upgrade its merchant support policies. Reducing merchant operating costs remains the central goal, continuing from 2025 strategy.
